sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Brandon, a male, presented with neck pain following multiple car crashes, the most recent being in February of this year.
He reports experiencing sharp, incapacitating pain, rating his pain at a 12 out of 10.
He has a history of previous car crashes and has undergone surgery.
He has also been experiencing headaches, dizziness, visual disturbances, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and muscle spasms.
He was previously prescribed fentanyl, but has not taken it in several weeks.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of multiple car crashes, including a recent one in February of this year, and has undergone surgery.
He has also been in therapy and has visited a pain clinic.
Review of systems: The patient reports experiencing headaches, dizziness, visual disturbances, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and muscle spasms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient exhibited pain on palpation of the neck, both on the bony process and muscle, with limited range of movement.
He was unable to bend his neck forward or backwards without pain.
There was no bruising, swelling, or laceration noted.
Test Results: An X-ray was performed, which did not show a fracture.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a neck sprain, which is causing his symptoms of pain, headaches, dizziness, numbness, and muscle spasms.
Problem list: Neck sprain; active.
Plan:
I prescribed Robaxin 1500mg every 6-8 hours as needed for pain.
I advised the patient to apply ice to the affected area when experiencing bruising and swelling.
I also instructed the patient to perform exercises to improve range of movement in the neck.
We will refer the patient to physical therapy after the MRI results are available.
I will also consider referring the patient to pain medicine for local injections if the Robaxin is not effective.
Follow-up: We will schedule a follow-up appointment after the MRI results are available to reassess the patient's condition and consider further treatment options.
